title: Wormhole: from sequences to the cosmos
subtitle: Follow a prediction idea until a wormhole opens onto the sky.

steps.title: Learning over time
steps.body: 1997. Long Short-Term Memory — a network that can hold on to information across long stretches of a sequence. The idea is prediction: given what came before, anticipate what comes next. Let's follow it and see where it ends up.

steps.title: Sequence to sequence
steps.body: By 2014 the idea has grown into reading one sequence and generating another — the RNN encoder–decoder, built for translation. Same core: consume a series, predict the next thing. Its details open on the left.

steps.title: Forecasting the future
steps.body: Keep going. By 2021 the thread reaches a synthesis — "Time-series forecasting with deep learning." We've stayed inside one idea the whole way: take a sequence, predict what comes next. This is where it gets strange.

steps.title: A wormhole
steps.body: Not every link is a citation. A violet conduit blooms from this node — a semantic edge to a near-identical idea that never cited it, in a field you'd never think to check. Where does it lead?

steps.title: Gravitational waves
steps.body: Astrophysics. "Deep neural networks to enable real-time multimessenger astrophysics" (2018). The same sequence-prediction idea that forecasts a stock ticker is here detecting gravitational waves the instant they wash over Earth — reading the cosmos in real time. A forecasting tool, alive in physics, and the citation graph would never have shown you.

steps.title: Through the wormhole
steps.body: Cross the semantic edge into the other field — the jump no citation chain ever offered. Kinship the graph found by meaning, not by reference.

steps.title: Found by exploring
steps.body: We started on a 1997 model of memory over time, followed it through translation and forecasting, and a wormhole dropped us somewhere no one was looking: catching gravitational waves from space. That's the point — you don't search for the surprise, you explore until it finds you.
